But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. programme.title: The Met: Policing London

However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from South Shields, Tyne and Wear, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville investigate whether cheaper alternatives will do exactly the same job as more expensive big brand medicines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of one man's claim for more than £300,000, when it reveals that he can do everything he claims he can't and there's 'paws for thought' as a pet owner refuses to believe her furry friend isn't covered by a pet insurance policy. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: The burglar who found he had taken on a whole posse of local crime-busting shopkeepers. Plus, the thief whose distinctive pair of trousers proved to be his undoing. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by two teams led by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper at an antiques fair in Newbury, Berkshire. The presenter also has a chat with Tim Weeks about the collectable toy market(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When Al gets cold feet, Jimmi suggests an unusual way to decide the fate of the Icon and Becky drops a bombshell on Daniel. Meanwhile, Ruhma is accused of negligence by the daughter of a patient, from 21 years earlier(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Businessman Mish, facilities manager Victoria and financial services consultant Simon choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin serves up some winning houses for tennis-playing town-seekers keen to test out Shaftesbury as their perfect Dorset town and have £460,000 to make the move. They quickly discover it's a top choice, and not just because it's on a hill but because of a great social scene, high street independents, festival events and stunning green spaces(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell scour the antiques shops around Sheffield to find something that will give them the edge at the auction. Natasha goes out of her comfort zone when considering some 18th-century pottery, but feels more at home when she spots something from the 1970s. Philip opts for some typically 'Serrell' items, as well as chewing over more prehistoric stuff. He also goes out of his way to prove that chivalry isn't dead. On the road, Philip visits a factory using the world-famous Sheffield steel to make some very fine tools, and Natasha heads to the woods for a spot of foraging and some free food. It's going to be a nail biter at their final auction at Lytham St Annes, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former England defender Stuart Pearce, snowboarder Aimee Fuller, featherweight boxing star Josh Warrington and Great Britain sprinter Jeanette Kwakye join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville's idea to open a new catering arm, offering his customers tasty toasties, doesn't quite go according to plan when his reconditioned toaster blows a fuse. Mr Newbold gets a little bolder in his pursuit of Mrs Rossi, while trying to avoid the Black Widow. Leroy and Willis meet Beth and Ruby with a view to going on a date, but Willis is worried that Ruby's not right for him. David Jason, James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Geoffrey Whitehead star in veteran writer Roy Clarke's much-loved comedy(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin thinks everything is over but Ben goes to extreme lengths to prove he is not be messed with. Leo ignores Callum's warning to stay away from Whitney and continues to charm her. Stuart starts work at Walford East, where Ian seizes the opportunity to embarrass him. Karen enlists Chantelle and Bernadette to make it up to Bailey, and Honey is surprised by Adam's behaviour towards Mariam and Arshad(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Watchdog presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Greg James join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Aquaman star Jason Momoa talks about his part in futuristic TV drama See, Game of Thrones star Emilia Clarke promotes her romantic comedy Last Christmas, while Oscar-winner Regina King discusses her HBO series Watchmen. Top comic Ross Noble also secures a spot on Graham's sofa. Plus, American-Cuban singer-songwriter Camila Cabello performs Liar(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire takes Fleabag to an all-female silent retreat. What should be a period of peaceful solitude is disrupted by some explosive secrets coming to light, a surprising connection with an old acquaintance, and an unusual neighbouring workshop. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Marcus Wareing, and guest Sir Lenny Henry, while Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues from a law firm in Aberdeen - Dara, Rachael, Emma and Jonny - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges. Senior solicitor Rachael hopes to impress with her red velvet cake, while Dara aims to win over the judges with his unicorn-themed cake, but will there be too much style over substance?(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker reviews the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Aston Villa and Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: Tonga v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the opening Test in a four-match series for the Lions, which took place at Waikato Stadium in Hamilton, New Zealand(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Shropshire to help a man look for a property on a £475,000 budget, and heads to Wenlock to try her hand at the Japanese pot-firing technique of raku(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the sixth live episode of this year's series. After David James and Nadiya Bychkova were eliminated last week, the 11 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home with their Halloween-inspired routines. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Vickie and Julie, sisters from Merseyside, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tension rises between Ethan and Rash when Ethan catches Rash making a personal call, but a potential argument between the two is interrupted when a patient starts coughing up blood and goes in to cardiac arrest. Meanwhile, Rosa opens up to David about her past, and Charlie's frustration grows over the lack of support for Duffy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Aston Villa at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between City and Villa since March 2016, when two goals from Sergio Aguero helped the Citizens record a comprehensive 4-0 victory. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Everton at the Amex Stadium, Watford v Bournemouth at Vicarage Road, West Ham United v Sheffield United at London Stadium, and Friday night's game between Southampton and Leicester City at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Minnesota Vikings v Washington Redskins at US Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform, including a chest of drawers and a collection of redundant file boxes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Compton Verney, Warwickshire, where finds include a sketch by illustrator Quentin Blake, the Duke of Wellington's riding crop and Queen Victoria's chocolate box. Christian Beadman gets his hands on a cricket stump from the infamous Bodyline series, and Fiona learns more about Compton Verney's fascinating folk art collection, which depicts everyday life during the 18th and 19th centuries(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives search for relatives of a woman from Lambeth, south London, for whom there is a major question mark over the value of her estate(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Top chef Jun Tanaka has his eye on the prize as he puts three home cooks through a series of culinary challenges, including how to make the perfect red wine sauce(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin presents the first of two programmes exploring animal relationships, finding out how they meet, mate and form relationships, and asking whether they can fall in love the same as humans. On her travels, she finds several animals that have discovered the secrets of successful long-lasting relationship, including the bonobo of the Congo, Africa's wild dogs and tamarins in the UK. But there are other sides to the story, like the devoted penguin couple that stayed together for 10 years, only for things to turn out not quite as they seemed. Liz also hears how a Siamaon gibbon dealt with the death of his life partner and meets a pair of elephants with an extraordinary friendship(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Examining the hunting methods of aquatic predators. The vastness of oceans means that wildlife is scattered over a large area, and animals often have to travel great distances in search of food. Blue whales' great size proves useful in their search for krill, while frigate birds have developed a clever means of catching food without having to land on water. Also featuring footage of sharks, sea lions, spinner dolphins, albatross and sargassum fish(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Clevedon, where artist Jon Webster and globetrotting retiree Caroline Crutchley learn the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal talks to some of the couples and looks at a few of Saturday's costumes with designer Vicky Gill. Plus, further progress on the contestants' training with Ian Waite(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his Grand Tour with a chilly swim across Loch Ba high on Rannoch Moor and struggles against the elements while trainspotting. He also meets some veteran Tunnel Tigers - men who tunnelled deep inside the Grampians, diverting water to hydroelectric schemes(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King mount their bikes for the final leg of their epic journey along the American road. They begin in Las Vegas, where they learn how to make apparently the best mashed potato in the world - inspiring them to cook a massive lobster and prawn cocktail. Back on the road they finally reach California, where they follow the path of the 49ers during the gold rush and learn about cooking with marijuana - which is perfectly legal in Los Angeles. They also enjoy the best tacos they have ever eaten at a taco festival, before reaching Santa Monica pier - the end of their trip(n)

programme.title: Giri/Haji
programme.desc: As Kenzo searches Saburo's apartment for clues to his brother's whereabouts, he comes face to face with an assassin who gives him a message from the mob boss Mr Abbot - go home or go to the morgue. But Kenzo is reluctant to leave London without Yuto, and after news from Tokyo that the body count is rising, he realises he has to find his brother before the gang war gets out of control. Besides, he has also begun to form other, more personal, attachments in the English capital. Crime thriller, starring Takehiro Hira and Kelly Macdonald(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools presents the second the long-running music show, introducing Liam Gallagher with songs from his second solo album Why Me? Why Not, featuring his son Gene playing drums on The River. Also performing are four-piece Australian rockers Amyl and the Sniffers, Surrey singer-songwriter Rex Orange County and rapper Kojey Radical, with tracks from his latest EP Cashmere Tears. The line-up is completed by soul singer Celeste, making her Later debut, and singer and podcast queen Jessie Ware, who also co-hosts tomorrow's longer show(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The crime documentary reveals how a 1970s miscarriage of justice, involving three teenage boys, led to a new method of police interrogation and investigation. When the body of Maxwell Confait was found in south London, three local boys confessed to arson and murder and were consequently convicted, despite retracting their statements and protesting their innocence. However, each of their convictions were eventually overturned, which shone a light on dangerous police practices that pressured the boys to confess to a crime they did not commit(n)

programme.title: Japan with Sue Perkins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue Perkins continues on her trip around Japan, exploring a nation caught between the demands of a hi-tech future and the pull of a traditional past. In the concluding episode, Sue travels to the ancient city of Kyoto to spend time with geishas and meets those working hard to keep geisha culture alive. She then heads south of Kyoto to experience free diving with Ama divers, before travelling to Hiroshima, where she meets Mr Tetsushi, an 84-year-old survivor of the atomic blast during the Second World War. Finally, Sue returns to Tokyo, to see how the Japanese are tackling challenges of the future(n)

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: The three artists travel to a home in Birmingham. It's an emotional brief from the mystery buyer, who has up to £750 to spend on a piece of art to reflect a family bond after donating half of her liver to her mother. Presented by Nick Knowles(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne rescues a collection of car exhaust parts, a workbench and a metal table frame, before he and two others restyle them in the hope of making a profit for the owners(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown takes a couple round the West Yorkshire market town of Wetherby, showing them properties for their £550,000 budget and also exploring the local cafes and horticulture(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A look at the role that plants and gardening play in people's mental and physical wellbeing, exploring the idea that gardening is a good and positive pastime. Arit Anderson travels to a medical centre in Kent where gardening is prescribed by doctors, to help patients cope with long-term mental and physical problems. Adam Frost is at a community garden in Oxfordshire, created to provide ongoing support for sufferers of Parkinson's disease. Back at Longmeadow, Monty Don plants bulbs for spring and catches up on some seasonal tasks(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Britain's bonds with its Celtic cousins across in Brittany are explored. Neil Oliver visits the province of Finistere, battered by some of the world's wildest waters(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: Heirs to the estate of a Sussex man learn about their grandfather and one relative receives a life-changing knock on the door. Plus, heirs to the estate of a popular Sussex man learn about their remarkable grandfather, a priest in the British Orthodox church(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: The weekly final sees chefs Nigel Haworth, Amandine Chaignot, Paul Ainsworth and Jun Tanaka pair up with their chosen home cook partners and go head to head in the ultimate pro-am cooking competition. And to pile on the pressure, three-star Michelin chef Pierre Koffmann judges their final dishes. Culinary challenge, hosted by Sheree Murphy(n)

programme.title: Animals in Love
programme.desc: In the second of two programmes about relationships in the wild, Liz Bonnin explores the ways in which animals get together. She observes a form of extreme flirting in a troop of capuchin monkeys in South America, which sees the females go to extraordinary lengths to attract the alpha male, reveals how African hyenas find it tough getting a girl and how an orang-utan in America chooses its partner in a very 21st-century way. For other animals, it turns out smell is a deal-breaker, while for others, it's all about having the best moves on the dance floor(n)

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: Examining animals on the hunt in deserts and grasslands, where prey has nowhere to hide - but neither do predators, making the element of surprise difficult to come by. Cheetahs use their unmatched speed to solve this problem, while lions have learned to use desert storms to conceal their approach and caracals are able to blend into the tall dry grass. The programme also features footage of bald eagles stalking snow geese, hot rod ants who rely on the sun to kill their food, and headlight beetles that lure termites to their death with an eerie glow(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Stratford-upon-Avon, where businessman Kamran Osman challenges behaviour consultant Neil Tomalin to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals, and there's a live music performance in the studio(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are Jan Morris' Pax Britannica trilogy, the musical Les Misérables, UK number one singles of the 1960s and The Vampire Chronicles by Anne Rice. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Weasels are often portrayed as villains and associated with bad behaviour. In an attempt to determine whether this assessment is correct, this documentary employs the use of over 50 hidden cameras to follow the adventures of first-time stoat mum Bandita as she raises her kits in a unique weasel wonderland. Cameras capture the creatures interacting, revealing the true nature of the weasel family - feisty and fearless. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: After William finds Adso and deduces the meaning of the verses of the Apocalypse on the library frescoes, he begins to suspect that Remigio and Salvatore were followers of Dolcino. When Adso asks for a book about the Dolcinians in the library, he arouses the suspicions and disapproval of Malachia and Benno who warn him to avoid such heretical texts. The papal delegation continues to advance, leaving death and destruction in its wake. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Damian Hardung star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren head to the foothills of the Swiss Alps to experience a very different type of travel - health and wellness tourism. The duo pay a visit to Grand Resort Bad Ragaz, which lies in the shadow of an ancient gorge, the thermal waters of which have lured visitors for centuries. The water is piped down to the hotel grounds to fill pools, pour out of drinking fountains and be used in the hotel's own beer(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals which start with a 'q', this time inviting Daliso Chaponda, Cariad Lloyd and Phill Jupitus to join regular panellist Alan Davies. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme, featuring soul singer Celeste and singer and podcast queen Jessie Ware as co-host. Liam Gallagher presents songs from his second solo album Why Me? Why Not, featuring his son Gene on drums. Plus, punk rockers Amyl and the Sniffers, Surrey singer-songwriter Rex Orange County, rapper Kojey Radical and soul singer Celeste(n)

programme.title: Albert Nobbs (2011)
programme.desc: A woman in 19th-century Ireland has lived in disguise as a man for 30 years, holding down a job as a butler at a Dublin hotel. She meets a married house painter whom she discovers is a fellow cross-dresser, and is inspired by her new friend's happy life to seek a wife of her own. Period drama, starring Glenn Close, Mia Wasikowska, Aaron Taylor-Johnson and Janet McTeer(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: He was the rock star of investing. Hundreds of thousands of people trusted him with their savings and pensions. But Neil Woodford's funds lost billions and now investors are waiting to find out how much of their money they will get back. So how can you know who to trust with your cash? Richard Bilton investigates the secretive world of fund managers - the people who gamble with your money(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: Marnie desperately wants to get scouted and needs Anton to let her play with the boys(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter and eco-activist David Attenborough joins Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss and earns his Green Badge(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: Tonga v Great Britain (Kick-off 8.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the opening Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which takes place at Waikato Stadium in Hamilton, New Zealand and is Great Britain's first game since 2007. Former England head coach Wayne Bennett is in charge of the Lions, and he will be hoping for a repeat of his side's 20-18 victory over Tonga in the 2017 World Cup semi-final. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena, including a robot designed by a nine-year-old girl, which comes complete with a huge pink axe. Other entrants include a team of students from Falkirk whose robot is made of wood and has a petrol engine, and a family team with an ingenious creation made up of parts sourced from their local scrapyard(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actresses Jennifer Saunders and Patricia Potter take to the road with experts Philip Serrell and Mark Stacey. They travel through Berkshire, Hampshire and Oxfordshire on a hunt for items to sell at an auction in Greenwich, south London(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher select items to take to auction, including a masonic watch and a sought after commemorative mug by Eric Ravilious. Plus, presenter Paul Martin visits the home of Britain's first Prime Minister, Robert Walpole(n)

programme.title: Laura (1944)
programme.desc: A woman presumed murdered turns up alive and well, confounding the suspicions of the New York detective working on the case, who thought the killer could have been one of her many socialite friends. Meanwhile, he finds himself irresistibly drawn to her as he tries to establish the victim's real identity. Mystery, with Gene Tierney, Dana Andrews, Clifton Webb and Vincent Price(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to transform a garden room in a three-bedroom Victorian terraced house in Shrewsbury for Mike and Lisa. Robert suggests a structure that gives each of them their own separate spaces, while Laura attempts to blend the needs of the whole family with in a highly adaptable garden room(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers at Calshot on the south coast race to the rescue of a 180-metre-long cargo ship which has run aground on a sandbank, and is now listing at a dangerous angle. At the most northerly lifeboat station on the British mainland, the crew gets called out to investigate a personal locator beacon which has been set off on a remote stretch of rocky coastline, and in Kinsale, volunteers come to the aid of a horse(n)

programme.title: Britain's Classroom Heroes
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Tina Daheley present highlights of the annual Teaching Awards from London's Roundhouse, Those presenting the trophies include broadcaster Joan Bakewell, gymnast Max Whitlock and Girls Aloud's Nicola Roberts(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Capt Mainwaring and the platoon offer to help Pte Godfrey's widowed friend gather in the harvest on her farm - but their well-meaning efforts end in predictable chaos. Comedy first aired in 1972, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Arnold Ridley(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Weasels are often portrayed as villains and associated with bad behaviour. In an attempt to determine whether this assessment is correct, this documentary employs the use of over 50 hidden cameras to follow the adventures of first-time stoat mum Bandita as she raises her kits in a unique weasel wonderland. Cameras capture the creatures interacting, revealing the true nature of the weasel family - feisty and fearless. Narrated by Julie Walters(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures Down Under to explore Australia by train. He begins by riding the legendary Ghan Railway, a train almost a kilometre in length and hauled by two locomotives, which covers nearly 2,000 miles across Australia's red centre. Michael traces its route from the southern harbour of Port Augusta to Darwin on the north coast, along the way meeting the indigenous people of Alice Springs, visiting a kangaroo sanctuary and joining Australians to mark the anniversary of Anzac Day, the First Word War battle that defined their nation(n)

programme.title: Westwood: Punk - Icon - Activist (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary film reflecting on the extraordinary career of fashion designer Vivienne Westwood, looking closely at her artistry, her activism and her cultural significance. Featuring contributions from Westwood herself, as well as Pamela Anderson, Christina Hendricks and Kate Moss(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Blanca senses that the ballroom scene is on the verge of breaking into the mainstream, and encourages her House of Evangelista children to follow their dreams. Angel pursues her ambition of becoming a fashion model, while Pray Tell joins an activist organisation fighting for the rights of HIV-positive people. Return of the drama, starring Billy Porter(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Blanca is determined to set a good example to her children and launches a business venture with her life's savings. But being a transgender businesswoman of colour, and from a poor background to boot, means the odds are stacked against her. Meanwhile, Elektra pulls a characteristically bold move, and Ricky has returned to town - and is back with Damon. But has distance made the heart grow fonder?(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the edition that first aired on August 25, with Big Country, Jane Wiedlin, Brother Beyond, Bomb the Bass, Womack and Womack, Status Quo, Breathe, Yazz & the Plastic Population and Tanita Tikaram Womack and Womack(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist examines the building blocks of life that have been taken from the Earth's elements, including the pure sulphur mined in the heart of a volcano in Indonesia. On a precipitous dam wall in Italy, mountain goats seek out the chemicals essential to their survival, while in a Japanese bay, Brian explains how the dazzling display of thousands of glowing squid shows how life has utilised the planet's chemistry for its own ends(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Quantum Physics
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili investigates the important yet perplexing theory of quantum physics, beginning with Albert Einstein's work which questioned the validity of the subject in the 1930s - with his ideas largely ignored until scientist John Bell found a way to test his concerns during the 1960s. The presenter then recreates this experiment - with shocking results(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of President Duterte's bloody campaign against drug dealers and addicts in the Philippines. Told with unprecedented access to people on both sides of the war, the film is shot in the style of a fictional crime thriller with a real-life journalistic investigation into a campaign of killings(n)

programme.title: Pain, Pus & Poison: The Search for Modern Medicines
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates mankind's earliest attempts to tackle infection and reveals how microbes are used to fight against disease. The programme also examines how scientists, chemists and doctors, from Louis Pasteur to Howard Florey, each contributed to these medical breakthroughs, and there is a look at the eradication of smallpox(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto studies the city during the 18th century, and its fascination with art and sex. Casanova became an overnight celebrity and courtesans openly plied their trade, while great art was produced by Canaletto and Canova, and music by Vivaldi. But the hedonism could not go on indefinitely as first syphilis then the might of Napoleon's armies took their toll upon the city(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The Shelbys are lured by the Italians into a cat-and-mouse chase on the street of Birmingham, where it becomes clear that Tommy has met his match. Tommy tries to console himself with a visit from an old flame, but it soon becomes clear that he cannot always get what he wants. As his factory lies idle, Tommy confronts the possibility that the Communists might win and he'll be deemed a traitor to his class. Meanwhile, Changretta prepares to spring another trap. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Tommy finds himself engaged in bloody battle with Luca Changretta and his gang. The family gather to find out what happened, but Lizzie has even greater news to break. Meanwhile, an army colonel has questions for Ada about her past as a Communist, and Jessie Eden confirms just how far she is prepared to go in pursuit of her cause. Sensing an opportunity to capitalise on his situation, Luca Changretta makes his way to London to present a plan to Alfie Solomons. Starring Cillian Murphy, Helen McCrory and Adrien Brody(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The night of the big fight arrives, and as the bell rings and the crowd goes wild, dangers lurk in the shadows for Tommy Shelby and his family, and Tommy soon discovers who he can truly trust. Meanwhile, as Changretta plays his final ace, he sets in motion a series of events that will change the Peaky Blinders forever. Starring Cillian Murphy, Adrien Brody, Tom Hardy, Helen McCrory, Charlie Murphy, Sophie Rundle, Paul Anderson, Kate Phillips, Ned Dennehy and Jack Rowan(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: The Live Lounge Show
programme.desc: Clara Amfo peeks behind the scenes of Radio 1's Live Lounge, the twice-weekly feature attracting an array of some of the biggest and best pop artists on the planet. Here, Haim and Lewis Capaldi feature alongside rapper Stormzy(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Examining the era between Buddy Holly's death in early 1959 and the Beatles landing at JFK in spring 1964, when rock 'n' roll seemingly calmed down, went uptown and got spun into teen pop. The genre fuelled the Motown sound in Detroit and sound-tracked guitar instrumental groups on the west coast, such as the Ventures and Beach Boys, as it helped birth increasingly polished pop sounds across the States. Contributors include Jerry Lee Lewis, Ben E King and Chubby Checker(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Mark Goodier an edition of the pop chart show first shown on September 1, featuring performances by Level 42, Guns N' Roses, Yello, a-ha, Bomb the Bass and Phil Collins(n)

programme.title: K-Pop Idols: Inside the Hit Factory
programme.desc: The K-Pop phenomenon is shaking up the pop world and for the first time ever, there is now a serious challenge to the West's domination of the global music industry. James Ballardie travels to South Korea to uncover the secrets behind this worldwide success story, finding out how, in just 20 years, the music industry in the country has come from obscurity to become a major player on the world stage(n)

programme.title: One-Hit Wonders at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of hits by artists that were hardly heard from again after one of their songs climbed to the pinnacle of pop, alongside an analysis of the 'one-hit wonder' phenomenon. Featuring Peter Sarstedt's 1969 track Where Do You Go To (My Lovely)?, Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da, Move Closer, by Phyllis Nelson, and You Get What You Give, by New Radicals(n)

programme.title: Marc Bolan: Cosmic Dancer
programme.desc: Intimate biography, narrated in the pop singer's own words, first shown to mark the 70th anniversary of his birth and the 40th of his death. With the dawn of the 1970s and the break-up of the Beatles, Bolan became the gender-bending glam rocker whose band T. Rex revitalised the British music scene. However, this film - incorporating hitherto unseen home movies shot by record producer Tony Visconti and Bolan himself - reveals a far more complex and driven figure whose life was cut short at the age of 29. Contributors include the singer's partner, Gloria Jones(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll America
programme.desc: Examining the era between Buddy Holly's death in early 1959 and the Beatles landing at JFK in spring 1964, when rock 'n' roll seemingly calmed down, went uptown and got spun into teen pop. The genre fuelled the Motown sound in Detroit and sound-tracked guitar instrumental groups on the west coast, such as the Ventures and Beach Boys, as it helped birth increasingly polished pop sounds across the States. Contributors include Jerry Lee Lewis, Ben E King and Chubby Checker(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the edition that first aired on August 25, with Big Country, Jane Wiedlin, Brother Beyond, Bomb the Bass, Womack and Womack, Status Quo, Breathe, Yazz & the Plastic Population and Tanita Tikaram Womack and Womack(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Mark Goodier an edition of the pop chart show first shown on September 1, featuring performances by Level 42, Guns N' Roses, Yello, a-ha, Bomb the Bass and Phil Collins(n)

programme.title: Tones, Drones and Arpeggios: The Magic of Minimalism
programme.desc: Part one of two. Musician and conductor Charles Hazlewood goes in search of minimalism, classical music's last great 'movement', which began on America's West Coast in the 1950s. Tracking down the pioneers of the genre, Charles explores La Monte Young's groundbreaking experiments with musical form that included notes held for exceptionally long periods, and drones inspired by Eastern classical music and Hindustani singer Pandit Pran Nath. Charles visits the Californian ranch of Terry Riley, and discusses the musician's revolutionary experiments with tape recording looping and phasing, along with early synthesizer sound(n)

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Richard Fortey investigates why islands are natural laboratories of evolution and examines the unique and remarkable species that live on them. He begins by heading to Hawaii to investigate how life colonises a newly formed island, and encounters honeycreeper birds whose development rivals that of Darwin's famous finches, as well as carnivorous caterpillars that now cannot eat leaves, and giant silversword plants that thrive in parched volcanic soil at heights of 10,000ft(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Police work to uncover the role of Ryan's elder brother Fouad in the money-laundering scheme, and Laure and Gilou make a breakthrough arrest. In French(n)

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: When Lily is upset to realise Matt is going out with cool girl Greta Masters, Jas plans a make-over slumber party to cheer her up. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. To spare Lily's feelings they pretend to like her new look, but are relieved the next day in school to see Lily returned to her normal, natural self. Lily has realised it's better to be(n)

programme.desc: Arsenal player Rachel Yankey and her team take part in a gripping penalty shootout. Meanwhile, Rozzie struggles to score against a team of stinky Skunks. Narrated by John Motson(n)

programme.title: CBeebies' Alice in Wonderland
programme.desc: Adaptation of Lewis Carroll's classic tale in which a young girl learns to use her imagination as she journeys through a fairy-tale land. Staged at the Wales Millennium Centre, Cardiff, with a cast featuring numerous CBeebies stars, including Justin Fletcher, Ben Faulks, Katrina Bryan, Phil Gallagher and Pui Fan Lee(n)

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig discovers what Halloween is and decides to dress up as a bobble bug monster for the party, but her friend Angel arrives in the same outfit(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Cook and Line find Captain HeyHo's old guitar and persuade her to play them a tune.(n)

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day goes on another time-travelling adventure to collect barnacles from a prehistoric ocean for his new museum display. Along the way, he tracks down a giant prehistoric whale(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is looking at useful lights(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: As Waffle and Gramps bake a doggy cake, Mrs Hobbs falls in love with a lost kitten(n)

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: The twins make their own wrapping paper for Vinda's birthday gift after it is delivered from the supermarket without any(n)

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy dresses up on the day of Vinda's knights and princesses party, but Tim and Tony do not want to join in the fun(n)

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from(n)

programme.title: Teacup Travels
programme.desc: Great Aunt Lizzie tells Charlotte the Adventure of the Ancient Headrest, the story of a man in Ancient Egypt who is worried sleeping will mess up his new hair style(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After Sarah helps Scarf Lady prepare the wool for her new roof, she settles down for a dreamy sleep(n)

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley goes to a farm in Northamptonshire to find out all about the world's biggest birds - ostriches. JB Gill discovers how cobnuts are grown and gathered in Kent(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Ghana, Tyra is scrubbed from head to toe by her mum and gets dressed up for church. In India, six-year-old Anushka, goes shopping for eggs with her grandad, and discovers where eggs really come from. And in Italy, seven-year-old Mario is put through a gruelling work-out at his weekly kick-boxing class by his teacher Vincenzo(n)

programme.title: Our Family Fun
programme.desc: Brothers Bobby and Lenny make a volcano from paper and glue(n)

programme.title: My Story
programme.desc: Mummy Nancy tells her daughter Yvonne about growing up in China, and together they meet some special visitors from the country(n)

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan visits a farm as workers prepare their pigs for a visit from the vet. Rory Crawford helps a vet with an examination on some amazing alpacas(n)

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: African folk tale, telling the story of why fleas jump(n)

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a cafe, whilst Mr Tumble and Aunt Polly get ready for the Tumbleton trifle competition(n)

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Andy Day focuses his attentions on the lives of tadpoles, koalas, kangaroos and ring tailed lemurs and how they have one thing in common - they like to hitch a ride(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns how the life cycle of a butterfly works and finds out how wallpaper is made(n)

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: It's the day of the kite festival and first up are Zip and Pop, who dazzle the crowd with their performance(n)

programme.title: Tree Fu Tom
programme.desc: A pirate game gets out of control(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children(n)

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Aimee and Ashley are joined by a Dixie the Dog, dressed as crafty spider, and Bobbles the Bear as the sweet and trusting fly for Mary Botham Howitt's tale The Spider and the Fly(n)

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari is at Barry's house for a playdate, but he won't let her play with his toys(n)

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: A man with a strange beard worries Tig, so Woolly tries to help her overcome her fears(n)

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Sam seems bored and is not enjoying her latest reading book. However, when Zainab shows off her scrapbook about her life's adventures, Sam is inspired to make a book of her own(n)

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: With her parents stay at the hospital on the mainland awaiting the delivery of the new baby, Katie looks after the family cat, Fabbydoo, but he mysteriously disappears(n)

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and Duck take an unexpected trip whilst on their way to buy some fudge(n)

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The gang members have been growing a pumpkin all through summer for the Halloween party at school, and everyone has decided what fancy dress to wear - except for Lola(n)

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: It seems all of Tee's friends have the brand new Mega Action Ape toy. Tee is desperate for one too, but his birthday isn't for ages. However, anything is possible when Mo gets creative(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: When a radio falls out of the sky, Tiny, Small and Baby Soup Dragon are fascinated by the sounds that come from it(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka cannot find any stones in the garden, so his friends help him. Narrated by Derek Jacobi(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The man who took on the insurance firm that overcharged his mother for 30 years, the problem of fake or counterfeit goods being sold online and advice on recycling mobile phones(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Steve Brown takes a couple round the West Yorkshire market town of Wetherby, showing them properties for their £550,000 budget and also exploring the local cafes and horticulture(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin promises Bex he will spend time with her that afternoon but his plans are ruined when Ben demands he does another job. So he takes a desperate course of action to free himself once and for all. Leo sets out to muddy Callum's name in his bid to get closer to Whitney, while Stuart struggles working for Kathy. Billy gives Jay a shoulder to cry on about his father and also shows concern for Honey, worried that his ex does not look well(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from South Shields, Tyne and Wear, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Angela Rippon and Julia Somerville investigate whether cheaper alternatives will do exactly the same job as more expensive big brand medicines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: Secret filming is the undoing of one man's claim for more than £300,000, when it reveals that he can do everything he claims he can't and there's 'paws for thought' as a pet owner refuses to believe her furry friend isn't covered by a pet insurance policy. A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: The burglar who found he had taken on a whole posse of local crime-busting shopkeepers. Plus, the thief whose distinctive pair of trousers proved to be his undoing. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by two teams led by experts Charles Hanson and David Harper at an antiques fair in Newbury, Berkshire. The presenter also has a chat with Tim Weeks about the collectable toy market(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: When Al gets cold feet, Jimmi suggests an unusual way to decide the fate of the Icon and Becky drops a bombshell on Daniel. Meanwhile, Ruhma is accused of negligence by the daughter of a patient, from 21 years earlier(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Businessman Mish, facilities manager Victoria and financial services consultant Simon choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin serves up some winning houses for tennis-playing town-seekers keen to test out Shaftesbury as their perfect Dorset town and have £460,000 to make the move. They quickly discover it's a top choice, and not just because it's on a hill but because of a great social scene, high street independents, festival events and stunning green spaces(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades scours the recycling centre in Altrincham, Greater Manchester, in search of three skip-bound castoffs he can rework and repurpose. Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell scour the antiques shops around Sheffield to find something that will give them the edge at the auction. Natasha goes out of her comfort zone when considering some 18th-century pottery, but feels more at home when she spots something from the 1970s. Philip opts for some typically 'Serrell' items, as well as chewing over more prehistoric stuff. He also goes out of his way to prove that chivalry isn't dead. On the road, Philip visits a factory using the world-famous Sheffield steel to make some very fine tools, and Natasha heads to the woods for a spot of foraging and some free food. It's going to be a nail biter at their final auction at Lytham St Annes, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: Stress levels are running high when rugby legend Gareth and wife Maureen face their first lock on the Montgomery Canal. Later, the duo enjoy a spell on a horse-drawn narrowboat, finally experiencing the tranquillity of near silence drifting through a pristine landscape without a roaring boat engine. At a section of canal that is still impassable, they learn of plans to restore the canal back to its former glory(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville's idea to open a new catering arm, offering his customers tasty toasties, doesn't quite go according to plan when his reconditioned toaster blows a fuse. Mr Newbold gets a little bolder in his pursuit of Mrs Rossi, while trying to avoid the Black Widow. Leroy and Willis meet Beth and Ruby with a view to going on a date, but Willis is worried that Ruby's not right for him. David Jason, James Baxter, Johnny Vegas and Geoffrey Whitehead star in veteran writer Roy Clarke's much-loved comedy(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin thinks everything is over but Ben goes to extreme lengths to prove he is not be messed with. Leo ignores Callum's warning to stay away from Whitney and continues to charm her. Stuart starts work at Walford East, where Ian seizes the opportunity to embarrass him. Karen enlists Chantelle and Bernadette to make it up to Bailey, and Honey is surprised by Adam's behaviour towards Mariam and Arshad(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Watchdog presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian and actor Miles Jupp and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Greg James join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Aquaman star Jason Momoa talks about his part in futuristic TV drama See, Game of Thrones star Emilia Clarke promotes her romantic comedy Last Christmas, while Oscar-winner Regina King discusses her HBO series Watchmen. Top comic Ross Noble also secures a spot on Graham's sofa. Plus, American-Cuban singer-songwriter Camila Cabello performs Liar(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Return of the dating show in which participants are stripped of their make-up and designer clothes and set up with several unlikely men. The first edition features Miss Wales 2016(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire takes Fleabag to an all-female silent retreat. What should be a period of peaceful solitude is disrupted by some explosive secrets coming to light, a surprising connection with an old acquaintance, and an unusual neighbouring workshop. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: In the fourth week of the competition, the candidates are challenged to create their own range of electric bikes, before hosting their own launch event to sell their bikes to some of the UK's biggest cycle retailers. As the task unfolds, half of the team head to Nottingham to develop their new bike, while the other half of the team stay in London to choose accessories and create marketing material. At their events, poor pricing strategies and forceful tactics prove problematic, and back in the boardroom, one candidate is sent home(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Vivek Singh and Marcus Wareing, and guest Sir Lenny Henry, while Jane Parkinson selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Colleagues from a law firm in Aberdeen - Dara, Rachael, Emma and Jonny - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges. Senior solicitor Rachael hopes to impress with her red velvet cake, while Dara aims to win over the judges with his unicorn-themed cake, but will there be too much style over substance?(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker reviews the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Manchester City v Aston Villa and Liverpool v Tottenham Hotspur in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: Tonga v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the opening Test in a four-match series for the Lions, which took place at Waikato Stadium in Hamilton, New Zealand(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Shropshire to help a man look for a property on a £475,000 budget, and heads to Wenlock to try her hand at the Japanese pot-firing technique of raku(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a musicals version of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Rufus Hound, Trevor Dion Nicholas, Ria Jones, Sophie Evans, Carley Stenson, John Owen-Jones, Jodie Prenger and Denise Van Outen(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the sixth live episode of this year's series. After David James and Nadiya Bychkova were eliminated last week, the 11 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home with their Halloween-inspired routines. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the third episode, Vickie and Julie, sisters from Merseyside, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tension rises between Ethan and Rash when Ethan catches Rash making a personal call, but a potential argument between the two is interrupted when a patient starts coughing up blood and goes in to cardiac arrest. Meanwhile, Rosa opens up to David about her past, and Charlie's frustration grows over the lack of support for Duffy(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Aston Villa at the Etihad Stadium and Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor. This was the first meeting between City and Villa since March 2016, when two goals from Sergio Aguero helped the Citizens record a comprehensive 4-0 victory. Plus, action from Brighton & Hove Albion v Everton at the Amex Stadium, Watford v Bournemouth at Vicarage Road, West Ham United v Sheffield United at London Stadium, and Friday night's game between Southampton and Leicester City at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Minnesota Vikings v Washington Redskins at US Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: Laid Bare
programme.desc: Helping people improve their health and fitness by pointing out the damage they are doing to themselves. First up is 35-year-old bodybuilder Darren, a father-of-two who looks like he's in great shape - he doesn't drink or smoke and he's been a dedicated bodybuilder since his teens. However, he's been taking anabolic steroids for 15 years and has already suffered side-effects like lumps on the chest and erectile dysfunction. While he can see these problems, he's increasingly worried about the damage he's done that he can't see. After seeking attention, Darren is given some truly shocking news(n)

Woodworking specialist Bruce Faulseit is on hand to give an old bookcase a new look, while upholsterer Ray Clarke attempts a creative makeover on a set of dining chairs. For his own project, Jay has a plant stand to spruce up(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell scour the antiques shops around Sheffield to find something that will give them the edge at the auction. Natasha goes out of her comfort zone when considering some 18th-century pottery, but feels more at home when she spots something from the 1970s. Philip opts for some typically 'Serrell' items, as well as chewing over more prehistoric stuff. He also goes out of his way to prove that chivalry isn't dead. On the road, Philip visits a factory using the world-famous Sheffield steel to make some very fine tools, and Natasha heads to the woods for a spot of foraging and some free food. It's going to be a nail biter at their final auction at Lytham St Annes, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Martin promises Bex he will spend time with her that afternoon but his plans are ruined when Ben demands he does another job. So he takes a desperate course of action to free himself once and for all. Leo sets out to muddy Callum's name in his bid to get closer to Whitney, while Stuart struggles working for Kathy. Billy gives Jay a shoulder to cry on about his father and also shows concern for Honey, worried that his ex does not look well(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how the critically acclaimed TV series Spotlight on the Troubles: A Secret History was made. Reporters and producers reveal how over the space of two years they tracked down new stories and found secret papers, while also persuading people to speak out(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: The documentary follows detectives from the Murder Investigation Team as they hunt down the down the attackers of a 30-year-old man who was beaten up after a night out in Greenwich and died six days later. Croydon CID are on the lookout for a serial burglar who has been caught on CCTV at a number of different spots, while in Westminster, response officers arrest a woman and take her into custody. However, all the cells are full at the nearest station, so they are forced to travel several miles and wait a number of hours before they can check her in(n)

A cheeky claimant involved a minor shunt unsuccessfully tries to cash in with a big claim, a mobile phone scammer puts on a convincing performance, and a bus passenger spots an opportunity to line his pockets after a mishap between a bus and a wheelie bin. programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v New Zealand (Kick-off 9.00am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening last-four match, which takes place at International Stadium Yokohama, and will see Eddie Jones' side face Steven Hansen's men. Both sides were impressive in their quarter-final matches a week ago, with England overcoming Australia 40-16 and the All Blacks dominating Ireland 46-14. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Sean Fitzpatrick, reports from Jill Douglas, Brian O'Driscoll and Maggie Alphonsi, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Lawrence Dallaglio(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, with celebrity contestants including professional skater Sylvain Longchambon, singer Joe McElderry, and Olympian snowboarder Aimee Fuller competing alongside members of the public. Hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara(n)

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. In the first episode, Paul meets seven staffie puppies whose mother has rejected them, before he visits a 13-year-old mongrel who was found wandering the streets of North London. Elsewhere, Paul lends a hand building a ladder and a platform for nine-year-old Archie(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as EastEnders star Charlie Brooks, paralympian Richard Whitehead, comedian Shappi Khorsandi and presenter David Dickinson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents the dating show, with a stripper, a farmer and a gymnast and Scarlett Moffatt's ex-boyfriend entering the 'love lift', and giving it their all in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date. But they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: The star-studded version of the singing contest continues with the first of the live shows, with Vinnie Jones joining those who made it through the auditions, including Ricki Lake, Jenny Ryan, Megan McKenna and the Love Islanders. With Simon Cowell mentoring the Groups, Louis Walsh coaching the Unders and Nicole Scherzinger in charge of the Overs, the celebrity hopefuls will be leaving their comfort zones at the door as they battle it out to ultimately win a record deal(n)

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined in the studio by Strictly Come Dancing judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, as well as former One Direction star-turned solo singer Liam Payne, comedian Nish Kumar, Queer Eye presenter Jonathan Van Ness and Brit award-winning Scottish singer-songwriter Tom Walker(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming(n)
