programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise becomes unnerved by the phone calls she is receiving. Deirdre is overwhelmed when Tracy returns home. Curly urges Elaine to stop being petty(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise is pestered with calls all night. Ken is thrilled to see Tracy and Deirdre back together again. Bet is upset to learn Alec has another lady friend(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: There has been foul play and Steve Marshall is the prime suspect. Nick is reluctantly placed in the limelight. Joe's prospects suddenly improve. Sangeeta's future seems less rosy(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cupid's arrow reaches its unlikely target, while some sharp words wound Turner. Surprise guests embarrass Sarah, and Lorraine finds herself disappointed(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress goes in search of a bogus water board official who has been robbing the elderly, but ends up investigating a suspicious death linked to one of the burglaries. Meanwhile, Blaketon seeks to rectify a miscarriage of justice by reviving a pre-war darts tournament between the Aidensfield Arms and the White Lion, and Gina begins to get cold feet about her impending marriage to Phil. Rural drama, starring Derek Fowlds and William Simons(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Suzy gives Stan food from the canteen to take home - but it's stolen property. When Blakey discovers that Stan and Jack are biting the hand that feeds them, he vows to make trouble. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Stephen Lewis(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack feel guilty about an accident in the bus, so they volunteer to care for the Inspector's pets while he is on holiday. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny and Dennis are forced to cut short their honeymoon in Spain, and Gina grows increasingly unhappy at the thought of having to give up her job as pub licensee to marry a policeman. Ventress creates a bad impression at the station, and a crooked travel agent finds a way of cashing in on local holiday-makers' absence, while Vernon organises a Bentley owners' rally at the hall. Sarah Tansey and Duncan Bell star(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris and Kim make a deal, and Pollard has a gift for Alice. Turner is missing from the Woolpack, while in Skipdale, Shirley cannot believe her eyes(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner's return scandalises the Woolpack, Rachel returns home to unwelcome news, while at Emmerdale, Robert has troubles of his own(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is moved to receive a letter from Buckingham Palace. Don is annoyed when Denise does not invite him to her birthday party. Bet is certain Steve is two-timing Vicky(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy is indignant when Bet stops her from drinking lager in the Rovers. Don refuses to go to the party as Ivy's there and spends the evening drinking with Bet(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jenny and Dennis are forced to cut short their honeymoon in Spain, and Gina grows increasingly unhappy at the thought of having to give up her job as pub licensee to marry a policeman. Ventress creates a bad impression at the station, and a crooked travel agent finds a way of cashing in on local holiday-makers' absence, while Vernon organises a Bentley owners' rally at the hall. Sarah Tansey and Duncan Bell star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A six-month-old boy dies in what appears to be a case of cot death, but when shaken baby syndrome is later revealed as the cause, Brooks and Devlin are left with a murder investigation that throws up many possible suspects. New evidence then comes to light during the trial, casting doubt over the prosecution's case, with Steel and Phillips forced into doing some detective work of their own. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

Sarah Tansey and Duncan Bell star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A flu epidemic hits the depot, improving Stan's financial health as he and Jack clock up overtime. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The new depot nurse moves in as the Butlers' lodger - causing problems for Arthur. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The girls find it tough sharing with one man, but their problems double once Robin's friend Larry moves in. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank's fears are confirmed by Lynn's revelation, Turner plucks up the courage to make a date, there is an unexpected candidate for a job, and a dinner party leaves Sarah envious(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel rebels over Joe's romance, there is mutiny in the Woolpack as Turner toes the line, but his foot is firmly down when Frank finally oversteps the mark(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don and Bet spend the night together, Denise accuses Don of making her life a misery, and Steve is upset to see Alison with another man(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel gets a job hostessing a party for a bathroom fittings company, Deirdre is horrified at the thought of having Craig around at Christmas. and Tanya sees Steve kissing Alison(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Taxi driver Eddie McEvoy gets more than he bargained for when he takes an asylum seeker to his new home, only to discover the accommodation is closed for the evening. Ever-helpful Eddie offers him a place to stay at his house - much to his wife's disgust - and despite the initial language problems, the two form an unusual bond. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Jamiu Adebiyi(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Domestic violence victim Yvonne tries to hide her husband Sean's abuse from friends and family. However, it's not long before they discover the truth, prompting her sister Kerry to turn up and demand that she leave the man who has made her life a misery for so long. Drama, starring Christine Bottomley and Lee Ingleby(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Ed is left in charge of the endangered-species project while Danny is away and Du Plessis is doing his community service. A beautiful stranger turns up at the clinic, claiming to have a lion that carries the rare Barbary gene, and she appears to take a romantic interest in him. However, Du Plessis grows suspicious and fears the newcomer has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Fatani struggles while helping Liv and Thabo with the animal census(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Everyone is shocked when a mystery woman arrives, claiming to be Ed's wife and seeking a reconciliation with him. However, a misunderstanding leaves her believing that Rosie is her estranged husband's new love interest. Meanwhile, a shocking link is made between some seemingly unrelated animal deaths. Jill Halfpenny (EastEnders, Waterloo Road) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Stan volunteer Olive as supervisor for the depot nursery. However, when she breaks her glasses and has to go home for a spare pair the clueless duo are left holding the fort. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Bob Grant and Anna Karen(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: One of Stan's colleagues is about to marry the Inspector's niece - but first he has to survive the stag night. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates the disappearance of a teacher from a secretive public school, and begins to question who he can trust when a body is discovered. Thursday wrestles with the imminent closure of Cowley Station as he tries to find the vital evidence that will connect Eddie Nero to a series of unsolved murders, and assigns Fancy to track down the man who may be the key to cracking the case(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star(n)

programme.desc: Raquel gets a job hostessing a party for a bathroom fittings company, Deirdre is horrified at the thought of having Craig around at Christmas. and Tanya sees Steve kissing Alison(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A series of scandals makes for an interesting few days in the quiet village of St Mary Mead, and most seem to revolve around a blustering local colonel, whose brusque manner and short temper make him unpopular with his fellow villagers. When he is found dead with a bullet through the head, Miss Marple and the reluctant DI Slack investigate. Whodunit, starring Geraldine McEwan as Miss Marple, with Jane Asher, Jason Flemying, Derek Jacobi and Miriam Margolyes(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Taxi driver Eddie McEvoy gets more than he bargained for when he takes an asylum seeker to his new home, only to discover the accommodation is closed for the evening. Ever-helpful Eddie offers him a place to stay at his house - much to his wife's disgust - and despite the initial language problems, the two form an unusual bond. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Jamiu Adebiyi(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Domestic violence victim Yvonne tries to hide her husband Sean's abuse from friends and family. However, it's not long before they discover the truth, prompting her sister Kerry to turn up and demand that she leave the man who has made her life a misery for so long. Drama, starring Christine Bottomley and Lee Ingleby(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Ed is left in charge of the endangered-species project while Danny is away and Du Plessis is doing his community service. A beautiful stranger turns up at the clinic, claiming to have a lion that carries the rare Barbary gene, and she appears to take a romantic interest in him. However, Du Plessis grows suspicious and fears the newcomer has an ulterior motive. Meanwhile, Fatani struggles while helping Liv and Thabo with the animal census(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Everyone is shocked when a mystery woman arrives, claiming to be Ed's wife and seeking a reconciliation with him. However, a misunderstanding leaves her believing that Rosie is her estranged husband's new love interest. Meanwhile, a shocking link is made between some seemingly unrelated animal deaths. Jill Halfpenny (EastEnders, Waterloo Road) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan believes living at home is cramping his style and resolves to find his own place. Vintage comedy with Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: One of Stan's colleagues is about to marry the Inspector's niece - but first he has to survive the stag night. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy investigate the death of amateur artist Ruth Fairfax, whose body has been discovered by Joyce - but the more they delve into the dead woman's background, the more mysterious she becomes. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, with guest appearances by John Sessions and Leslie Phillips(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a woman is drowned in a bath and made to look like a bride, with a chilling lipstick message on the bathroom mirror, it triggers a spate of ghoulish wedding-themed murders in Great Worthy. The case takes Barnaby and Jones to a donkey sanctuary, a heritage steam railway and a pub run by an ex-policeman and former brothel madam as they try to identify the killer. Guest starring Pam Ferris (Rosemary & Thyme) and Kacey Ainsworth (formerly EastEnders' Little Mo)(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse investigates the disappearance of a teacher from a secretive public school, and begins to question who he can trust when a body is discovered. Thursday wrestles with the imminent closure of Cowley Station as he tries to find the vital evidence that will connect Eddie Nero to a series of unsolved murders, and assigns Fancy to track down the man who may be the key to cracking the case(n)

programme.title: Goals of the 80s
programme.desc: A selection of the finest goals scored in the 1980s(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon visits the West Indies, but his excursion turns into an investigation into the murder of an old friend. Sixties drama, starring Roger Moore(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo suspects a murdered car thief who claimed to be coming into money is involved in an imminent heist - but has a week to find out where. Guest starring Ken Kercheval (Dallas)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner investigates the deaths of two patients at a sanatorium, suspecting that someone may be performing mercy killing on patients that are in pain(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry is pleased when villainous old friend Jack ''Oily' Wragg returns to England, However, his pleasure melts away when he learns that there are old scores to settle and that Wragg intends to stage one final heist. Light-hearted crime drama, guest starring Pete Postlethwaite, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar poses as a Russian secret police chief to catch an assassin who has claimed the lives of 13 intelligence agents. Starring Roger Moore, Glynn Edwards and Burt Kwouk(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A lawyer is murdered while working undercover investigating corruption in a major urban redevelopment project, and Theo uses some unexpected tactics to bring his killers to justice(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A tricky case involving homicide and insurance fraud puts Quincy at loggerheads with his oldest adversary, the country's top pathology expert, Doctor Herbert Stone(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held by the police after a car he is importing from Europe is impounded by drugs officers, so Terry goes in search of those he suspects are responsible for his boss' predicament - the vehicle's original owners. Light-hearted crime drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle learn an assassin has been hired to murder one of the delegates at an anti-terrorist conference and set about discovering the identity of the killer's target. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley is joined by Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little for the first day of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring eight first-round matches, including Michael van Gerwen v Ross Smith, James Wade v Jonny Clayton and Rob Cross v Darren Webster. Wade was the victorious player in Dortmund last year, overcoming home player Martin Schindler in the first round, and defeating the likes of Gerwyn Price and Max Hopp en route to the final, where he prevailed against Simon Whitlock. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling(n)

programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove try and break into the classic tractor market(n)

programme.desc: A Confederate soldier refuses to surrender at the end of the Civil War, and is branded a dangerous outlaw. Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Chief Dan George and Sondra Locke(n)

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley is joined by Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little for the afternoon session on day two of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring the concluding first-round matches, including Ian White v Michael Smith and Mensur Suljovic v Vincent van der Voort. programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley is joined by Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little for the evening session on day two of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring the opening four second-round matches. Should he have defeated Ross Smith in his first-round match, top seed Michael van Gerwen will be involved tonight against either Glen Durrant or Ricky Evans, and will be looking to avoid a repeat of this stage last year, when he was beaten 10-7 by Steve West. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: Crank (2006)
programme.desc: An assassin faces a race against time when he learns a rival has poisoned him with a drug that will kill him if his heart rate drops below a certain level. As he sets out to take revenge on his enemies, he has to resort to increasingly desperate acts to keep his adrenaline pumping. Action thriller, with Jason Statham, Amy Smart and Jose Pablo Cantillo(n)

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole visits the Isle of Man, the spiritual home of motorcycle racing, drops in to the Ace Cafe - the famous pitstop on the North Circular - and brings the restoration of his BSA Goldstar to a glorious climax(n)

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey return with the competitive angling show, with the first edition pitting comedian Bobby Davro against actor Ricky Groves. They head to the tranquillity of a fishing complex in Surrey, where they target crucians, bream, tench and carp(n)

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner go on a Croatian adventure, searching for huge king carp, culminating in them visiting the home of the Croatian carp record of 94lb(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Rishi Persad and the team look ahead to the day's racing at Cheltenham, where jumps racing returns, and there is also a preview of racing at Doncaster(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove try and break into the classic tractor market(n)

programme.title: The Car Chasers
programme.desc: The team considers setting up shop in California as Jeff and Meg travel to LA to check out potential sites. Once there, Jeff is unable to resist the chance to make the trip pay for itself by trying to snap up a couple of cars - a Lamborghini Countach and a Porsche Speedster(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage from today's race meetings at Cheltenham, Doncaster and Newbury. From Cheltenham: The 2.00 Matchbook Betting Exchange Handicap Steeplechase, 2.35 Masterson Holdings Hurdle Race, 3.10 Randox Health Handicap Steeplechase and 3.45 Pertemps Network Handicap Hurdle. From Doncaster: The 1.45 Doncaster Stakes, 3.25 Vertem Futurity Trophy Stakes and 4.00 Doncaster Handicap Stakes. Plus, two races from Newbury. With analysis by Mick FitzGerald and Luke Harvey, reports by Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman and Rishi Persad, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The secret of producing a classic Drumstick lolly at Britain's biggest family-owned sweet factory, and the making of guitars in demand from the likes of Dolly Parton and Ed Sheeran. Plus, how a roll of steel is turned into a finished radiator in a matter of minutes, and a company combining robotics and traditional artistry to create shotguns(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Jill Douglas presents highlights of the opening semi-final, which took place at International Stadium Yokohama. With commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Lawrence Dallaglio(n)

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of day three of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring the concluding four second-round matches. This stage of last year's tournament saw a huge upset, as top seed Michael van Gerwen was beaten 10-7 by Steve West, while Peter Wright was another high-profile casualty. Rob Cross demolished Richard North 10-2 to reach the quarter-finals, while another Englishman, Darren Webster, narrowly avoided an upset against Spaniard Cristo Reyes, edging through 10-9. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling, and analysis by Alan Warriner-Little and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Scarface (1983)
programme.desc: Small-time crook Tony Montana emigrates from Cuba to the US, determined to become rich and powerful. Admitted into the inner circle of a Miami drug lord, he ultimately takes over his boss's empire and even his wife. However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia(n)

With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage from today's race meetings at Cheltenham, Doncaster and Newbury. From Cheltenham: The 2.00 Matchbook Betting Exchange Handicap Steeplechase, 2.35 Masterson Holdings Hurdle Race, 3.10 Randox Health Handicap Steeplechase and 3.45 Pertemps Network Handicap Hurdle. From Doncaster: The 1.45 Doncaster Stakes, 3.25 Vertem Futurity Trophy Stakes and 4.00 Doncaster Handicap Stakes. Plus, two races from Newbury. With analysis by Mick FitzGerald and Luke Harvey, reports by Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman and Rishi Persad, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The secret of producing a classic Drumstick lolly at Britain's biggest family-owned sweet factory, and the making of guitars in demand from the likes of Dolly Parton and Ed Sheeran. Plus, how a roll of steel is turned into a finished radiator in a matter of minutes, and a company combining robotics and traditional artistry to create shotguns(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Jill Douglas presents highlights of the opening semi-final, which took place at International Stadium Yokohama. With commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Lawrence Dallaglio(n)

programme.title: Darts: European Championship Live
programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of day three of the event from the Lokhalle in Goettingen, Germany, featuring the concluding four second-round matches. This stage of last year's tournament saw a huge upset, as top seed Michael van Gerwen was beaten 10-7 by Steve West, while Peter Wright was another high-profile casualty. Rob Cross demolished Richard North 10-2 to reach the quarter-finals, while another Englishman, Darren Webster, narrowly avoided an upset against Spaniard Cristo Reyes, edging through 10-9. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and John Rawling, and analysis by Alan Warriner-Little and Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Scarface (1983)
programme.desc: Small-time crook Tony Montana emigrates from Cuba to the US, determined to become rich and powerful. Admitted into the inner circle of a Miami drug lord, he ultimately takes over his boss's empire and even his wife. However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk looks at the creation of the sewer system, delving into the archives to reveal how appalling sanitary conditions forced the city of Liverpool to take action. He descends underground to see the first groundbreaking sewer for himself, and hears about how the pioneering concept was built. He then heads to Stoke-on-Trent to see how the fashion for flushing toilets helped improve health and sanitation in Victorian homes. Mass production of ceramic toilets also became big business, creating many brands names still in existence today(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Crafting
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley, Rosie Wolfenden and four passionate hobby crafters head to the Haddon Hall Craft Fair in Bakewell, Derbyshire. Brian loves turning old furniture into new pieces, so Rosie offers tips on how to photograph and present his pieces for sale. James and Bridget have an alpaca farm and make yarn by spinning. Bridget learns about natural dyes, while James rushes to get production running with his sock-making machine. Mum-of-two Tracey is a paper crafter who makes original pieces for friends and family, but she urgently needs advice on pricing(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House

Midia is a bus driver and her husband Sacha a student with dreams of becoming an inventor of labour-saving devices, which he hopes to sell across the world. Will a week at the home of a successful entrepreneur inspire Sacha to pursue his dreams, or to reassess his business strategy?(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The stories of six epic train journeys, seen through the characters who travel, work and live near them. The first programme rides through Canada's iconic Rocky Mountaineer train in a jaw-dropping loop from Vancouver to the heart of the Rockies, through some of North America's most staggering natural beauty, and on to Banff in Alberta. The journey also ventures through the spiral tunnels and shows how to track bears with the National Park rangers. Narrated by Bill Nighy(n)

programme.desc: The former Pontins bluecoat heads to north devon to stay in a static caravan on a park owned by of one of the biggest holiday park chains in the UK. The park boasts its own entertainment centre, swimming pool, nature reserve and even its own private beach. He is later joined by one of his oldest friends who, just like him, is a former holiday park entertainer. Peter joins Shane on a visit to a theme park with a difference, and the duo attend a D-Day re-enactment at a local beach, complete with vintage jeeps and aircraft(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Shocking Ads with Shane Richie

He also opens up the archives to show vintage commercials from yesteryear. programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: Documentary examining the history of the airport, which opened in 1946 as London Airport and has developed into one of the busiest in the world. The film also reveals how many baggage handlers have now been replaced with robots, and how the air traffic control tower has become the most advanced in the world, using cameras that can pierce fog and artificial intelligence to collate flight data(n)

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell investigates the history of ocean liner the SS Queen Mary, finding out how the largest ship of its day could also become the fastest - achieving speeds that few large ships can match even 80 years later. On the passenger decks, he savours the art deco craftsmanship that made the vessel the much-loved 'floating gentleman's club', where royalty rubbed shoulders with movie stars(n)

programme.title: 10 Mistakes That Sunk the Titanic
programme.desc: Documentary examining the chain of events that led to the sinking of the ship April 14, 1912, when it struck an iceberg in the north Atlantic and went down with around 1,500 passengers and crew. From the procedure for fixing rivets into the ship's bows, the delays in its construction that pushed the maiden voyage into perilous iceberg season, and the astonishing fact that the lookouts on watch that fateful night had no access to binoculars, this film reveals the 10 key mistakes that conspired to sink the supposedly unsinkable Titanic(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Vicky Pattison narrates a countdown of disastrous incidents on reality shows, including Lee Otway's breakdown on Celebrity Love Island and a sex tape featuring one of the stars of Ex on the Beach. With contributions from Sean Fletcher, Alison Hammond, Vogue Williams, Nikki Grahame, Bianca Gascoigne, Paul Danan and Jessie Cave(n)

programme.title: Support Your Local Gunfighter (1971)
programme.desc: A slick conman tries to pass his dim-witted sidekick off as a notorious gunslinger as a way of profiting from a dispute between rival companies in a small mining town. Comedy Western, starring James Garner, Jack Elam, Suzanne Pleshette, Harry Morgan and Chuck Connors(n)

programme.title: The Last Wagon (1956)
programme.desc: A mixed-race renegade is convicted for the murder of his wife's killers and transported to prison in a wagon train. When the party is attacked by Apaches, the only survivors are the prisoner and a group of orphaned children, so he takes responsibility for leading them to safety. Western, with Richard Widmark, Felicia Farr, Ray Stricklyn and Susan Kohner(n)

programme.title: The Gunfight at Dodge City (1959)
programme.desc: A gambler becomes the sheriff of a frontier town, planning to use his position to avenge his brother's murderers - but the arrival of a face from the past muddies the water, and leaves him doubting the identity of the killer. Western, starring Joel McCrea, Julie Adams, John McIntire, Nancy Gates and Richard Anderson(n)

programme.title: A Night to Remember (1958)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama recreating the sinking of the Titanic, told from the point of view of the ship's second officer as he struggles to get as many passengers and crew off the vessel before it sinks. Meanwhile, the captain of a nearby vessel embarks on a doomed rescue mission. Starring Kenneth More, Honor Blackman, Ronald Allen, Tucker McGuire and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: Men in Black II (2002)
programme.desc: Agent J investigates the unauthorised landing of a spacecraft and discovers a malevolent alien disguised as a lingerie model is on the verge of destroying humanity. His only hope of stopping her diabolical plot is to restore the memory of his retired partner K - but his former mentor takes more than a little convincing. Sci-fi comedy sequel, directed by Barry Sonnenfeld, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Rosario Dawson, Lara Flynn Boyle, Johnny Knoxville, Rip Torn and Tony Shalhoub(n)

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011)
programme.desc: Secret agent Ethan Hunt and his team are implicated in a bombing at the Kremlin, forcing the American president to declare them fugitives. The spies plan to clear their name by abducting the real terrorist, which leads them into a pursuit around the globe. However, Hunt cannot be sure his colleagues are trustworthy. Action adventure sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Simon Pegg and Paula Patton(n)

programme.title: St Vincent (2014)
programme.desc: A lonely 12-year-old boy gets locked out of his house, so turns to his next-door neighbour, a misanthropic Vietnam veteran with a gambling addiction. The neighbour takes a shine to the youngster, and offers his services as a babysitter, providing valuable life lessons and an introduction to the joys of gambling, fighting and petty theft. Comedy drama, starring Bill Murray, Jaeden Lieberher, Melissa McCarthy and Naomi Watts(n)

programme.title: Paper Towns (2015)
programme.desc: A teenager enjoys a night of adventures with a mysterious girl, whom he has been fascinated with since childhood. The next day she disappears without trace, but he discovers she has left him a series of clues, which he and his friends follow in the hope of finding her. Romantic mystery based on the novel by John Green, starring Nat Wolff and Cara Delevingne(n)

programme.title: All That Heaven Allows (1955)
programme.desc: A wealthy New England widow breaks all the taboos of her conservative social circle by falling madly in love with her younger gardener. Her friends and grown-up children are scandalised as she contemplates marrying him, leaving her with a dilemma - to either bow to convention or take a chance at being happy. Romantic drama, with Jane Wyman, Rock Hudson, Agnes Moorehead and Conrad Nagel(n)

programme.title: Warlock (1959)
programme.desc: A ruthless vigilante is recruited as a sheriff to clean up the streets of a crime-ridden town, but his strong-arm tactics soon antagonise the townsfolk. When a woman turns up and accuses the lawman of murdering her fiance, the residents rally to her cause with the help of a rival gunslinger. Western, starring Henry Fonda, Richard Widmark, Anthony Quinn, DeForest Kelley and Dorothy Malone(n)

programme.title: Appointment with Danger (1950)
programme.desc: A nun is targeted by two murderers when they realise she is the only witness to one of their killings, and it is up to postal inspector to protect her and bring the men to justice. Noir thriller, starring Phyllis Calvert, Alan Ladd and Paul Stewart(n)

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey(n)

programme.title: Footloose (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager from Boston moves to a small conservative town, and discovers the council and a preacher have enforced a ban on loud music and dancing. He openly defies the harsh rules, revitalising the local youth and winning the heart of the reverend's daughter. Remake of the 1984 drama, with Kenny Wormald, Julianne Hough and Dennis Quaid(n)

programme.title: Spy (2015)
programme.desc: A deskbound CIA agent provides valuable background support for a dashing secret agent, but has never gone into action herself. Everything changes, when the spy mysteriously vanishes, and she leaps into action to take his place, infiltrating the inner circle of a ruthless arms dealer and trying to avert a global catastrophe. Comedy adventure, starring Melissa McCarthy, Jason Statham and Rose Byrne(n)

programme.title: Fright Night (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager realises his new neighbour is a vampire, but cannot convince anyone of the danger they are in. He turns to a foul-mouthed magician with a horror-themed Las Vegas show to help him slay the bloodsucker before he murders the entire neighbourhood. Comedy horror remake, with Anton Yelchin, Colin Farrell, David Tennant and Toni Collette(n)

programme.title: Goat (2016)
programme.desc: New college student Brad is desperate to fit into his new surroundings, so taking a cue from his older brother, he decides to pledge a fraternity. All goes well at first as he attends parties and meets several girls, but as he enters into the final stretch of the pledging ritual-known as hell week, Brad finds things taking a violent turn as he has to endure a series of brutal initiation rituals. Fact-based drama, with Ben Schnetzer and Nick Jonas(n)

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams(n)

programme.title: Horton Hears a Who! (2008)
programme.desc: A kindly elephant hears a cry of help coming from a speck of dust and discovers the minuscule city of Whoville. As he tries to help its citizens, the other animals begin to question his sanity, and the evil Kangaroo incites them to turn against the do-gooder. Animated adaptation of Dr Seuss's book, with the voices of Jim Carrey, Steve Carell, Carol Burnett, Seth Rogen and Will Arnett(n)

programme.title: Shark Tale (2004)
programme.desc: Ambitious fish Oscar dreams of escaping his humdrum undersea life. His wish comes true when a gangster shark keen to escape a world of organised crime seeks his help in faking his own death. But Oscar soon finds himself out of his depth as his fearsome reputation gets out of control. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Smith, Robert De Niro, Martin Scorsese, Renee Zellweger, Angelina Jolie and Jack Black(n)

programme.title: Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters (2013)
programme.desc: The modern-day teenage son of the Greek god Poseidon must lead his friends on a perilous journey into the Bermuda triangle. They need to recover the legendary Golden Fleece to save the world, but getting to it means braving all manner of terrifying monsters. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Logan Lerman and Alexandra Daddario(n)

programme.title: Super 8 (2011)
programme.desc: A boy and his friends sneak out at night to film an amateur zombie movie, only to witness a train derailment. As people start disappearing from their town and the military descends in force, they come to realise a dangerous alien was being transported on the train and is now on the loose. Sci-fi thriller, starring Joel Courtney and Elle Fanning(n)

programme.title: Deadpool (2016)
programme.desc: A fast-talking former mercenary develops terminal cancer, and agrees to be the subject in a shady experiment in the hope of a cure. The procedure leaves him horribly disfigured, but also possessing superhuman abilities which he immediately puts to use in a quest for revenge on the man responsible for his hideous appearance. Superhero comedy adventure, starring Ryan Reynolds and Morena Baccarin(n)

programme.title: Terminator Genisys (2015)
programme.desc: A soldier fighting in a post-apocalyptic war against living machines is sent back in time to prevent an attempt to change the past - but when he arrives, he finds that history has been changed beyond recognition - and that a plot is being hatched by an unexpected enemy to destroy the future of the human race. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Emilia Clarke(n)

Comedy, guest starring Christine Baranski(n)
