High-heel wearing Paolo is back for a second shot at love, meeting fellow Italian Mario, while single mum Claire is hoping Jordanian-born care worker and fellow divorcee Fadi might be the soulmate she is looking for(n)

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers
programme.desc: Pash gets rid of Bev's shocking naked self-portrait, Sketch helps Rich forget a marriage proposal gone wrong, and Uzzi takes on Chris's embarrassing self-inking. Meanwhile, Pash covers Caroline's foul-mouthed tatt in memory of a friend, Sketch conceals Donna's risky reminder of a new experience, and Alice creates a design for John after a life-threatening spider bit(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to hide the fact he had dinner with Penny after she broke up with Leonard, hoping to stay friends with her. However, keeping secrets does not prove to be his strong point, and it isn't long before his deceit is exposed. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon invites a famous physicist to the flat after befriending her online, but is put out to find Leonard showing an interest in her. Meanwhile, Penny has to confront her ex-boyfriend when the visitor turns up in his bedroom. Guest starring Judy Greer (Glenn Martin, DDS)(n)

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Tony visits Ted's flat to ease his guilty conscience over Stella choosing him, and makes a significant decision regarding his relationship. Meanwhile, Barney tries to get caught speeding after Marshall and Robin manage to successfully talk their way out of paying fines(n)

programme.title: Young & Hungry
programme.desc: When Josh has an issue with his therapist, he considers ending his sessions. Elliot and Alan make one last attempt to take their honeymoon, but they need Yolanda to come along and share the cost. Sofia gets another chance at love when she's asked out by a cute guy from her spin class(n)

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Jamie is feeling down after a serious injury to his foot following a dance rehearsal, and his mood is not helped when he finds out Harry has been advising Sam against forgiving him(n)

programme.title: Spencer, Vogue and Wedding Two
programme.desc: Documentary following Spencer Matthews and Vogue Williams as they plan their London wedding for friends and family. In the first edition, the pair put party planners through their paces, while Vogue takes mum Sandra and friend Millie Mackintosh wedding dress shopping(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Party-loving Jake plans his wedding to image-conscious bride-to-be Rosie, who dreams of a more intimate affair(n)

programme.title: Garfield 2: A Tale of Two Kitties (2006)
programme.desc: The lazy moggy stows away in his owner's luggage and ends up in London. He is promptly mistaken for an English upper-class cat, who has recently inherited a grand estate, and moves into his stately home - much to the dismay of the real aristocratic feline's conniving relation, who is bent on swindling him out of his money. Family comedy, starring Breckin Meyer, Billy Connolly and Jennifer Love Hewitt, with the voices of Bill Murray and Tim Curry(n)

programme.title: Rio 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Parrots Jewel and Blu lead a happy life together in Rio with their three chicks, until they find themselves hurled into an expedition up the Amazon. The journey leads to an encounter with an old enemy, while Blu also has to win the approval of Jewel's fearsome father. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jesse Eisenberg, Anne Hathaway and Jamie Foxx(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel does her best to get the children excited about Halloween. Meanwhile, Lewis asks Joe for permission to spend one last evening with his kids before he goes to jail. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel gets a strange visit from her doctor, who drops by at Halloween to try to convince her that she was once a powerful teenage witch. At first she and Joe don't believe it, but then she accidentally turns Lennox into a cat, and Joe's Halloween robot Scary Larry comes to life. Return of the comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray coaches Barry for his first high school wrestling match but keeps it a secret from Beverly, who is sure to disapprove of her son taking part in such a dangerous sport. Adam blackmails Erica into waiting in line with him to buy tickets for the new Stars Wars movie(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray gets his moment in the spotlight when he is honoured by his old high school for his basketball prowess. While he and Beverly are away from home, Barry takes advantage of their absence to throw a party to impress his school friends(n)

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: Meemaw refuses to drive Sheldon to a lecture so he takes matters into his own hands. Georgie tries to impress Veronica with his new business venture(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon and Raj organise a stag party for Howard, but stories about his sexual history are unwittingly revealed, and he has to persuade bride-to-be Bernadette not to call off the wedding. Comedy, starring Simon Helberg and Kunal Nayyar(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard tries to use his wedding as an excuse to avoid being sent into space, but ends up accepting the mission. Amy plots to get Sheldon to take the next step in their relationship, while Leonard accidentally asks Penny to marry him(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig present the semi-final of the baking contest, which tests the four remaining bakers' skills with patisserie. There are only three places in next week's final, and this round of challenges will determine who does not make the cut. The signature dish requires a delicate dome and a perfect finish, while for the technical, the bakers must take croquembouche to the next level. Finally, for the showstopper, they must use all their skills to construct an edible display case(n)

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Cornwall-based husband and wife Phil and Christine produce a five-foot long lemon and elderflower cake designed to look like a leaping fish(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: As Howard and his fellow astronauts sit in the space shuttle awaiting launch, he reflects on the tumultuous events of the previous few days, when he and Bernadette tried to get married. Meanwhile, his friends witness the blast-off on TV at home(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Despite being on the International Space Station, Howard gets caught in a strange argument between Bernadette and his mother via video link. Elsewhere, Raj feels lonely while his best friend is away and ends up interfering with Sheldon's and Leonard's dates. Comedy, starring Simon Helberg(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny tells the girls she is unsure about her relationship with Leonard and is considering breaking up with him, but when Sheldon finds out, he tries his best to change her mind. Meanwhile, on the International Space Station, Howard is being bullied by the other astronauts. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon hires an attractive female assistant, but when she becomes interested in Leonard, Penny decides to mark her territory by sleeping with him. Meanwhile, Howard starts to lose his mind when his return from space is delayed another week. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard returns from his space mission, but the welcome from his friends and family does not meet his expectations. His night alone with Bernadette goes badly, his mother ignores him, and he realises Stuart has taken his place as Raj's best friend. On top of that, Sheldon, Amy, Leonard and Penny kick him out of their group games night. Comedy, starring Simon Helberg(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Stuart hosts a Halloween party at the comic-book store, and Howard talks incessantly about his work on board the International Space Station, annoying his wife and friends. Penny and Leonard have a steamy encounter at the lab. Guest starring real-life astronaut Buzz Aldrin(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon upsets guest star Stephen Hawking when they play an online game of Words with Friends, until Howard advises him to let the scientist win. Elsewhere, Penny enrols in a college class, but decides to keep it a secret from Leonard. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg(n)

programme.title: Taken 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Former CIA agent Bryan Mills and his ex-wife Lenore are taken hostage in Istanbul by an Albanian gangster, whose son Bryan killed while bringing down a kidnapping ring. He is able to escape with the aid of his daughter, and together they fight to rescue Lenore and wipe out the criminal family. Action thriller sequel, starring Liam Neeson, Maggie Grace and Famke Janssen(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Programmes including This Morning, Naked Attraction, The X Factor, Bodyguard, Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain, Panorama: Inside No 10: Deal or No Deal?, Stacey Dooley: Face to Face with Armageddon, and The Circle come under the gaze of the armchair critics, as they share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Steven Weald and Sue Upson search for a winter home north of Naples on Florida's Gulf Coast, and shows the active couple five properties that are within their £150,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Entrepreneur and sports fishing enthusiast Ian Walls from Edinburgh has decided to expand his property portfolio by purchasing a place on Grand Bahama where he can spend a couple of months every winter. With his US-based girlfriend Lisa on hand to offer a second opinion, Sara Damergi shows them a selection of homes for sale around his £111,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Margie and Grayson live in their dated house at Jindalee in Queensland with their two daughters. It was a prize home in 1969, but now Neale Whitaker has the unenviable task of bringing the dodgy decor up to date, while Andrew Winter must find a family property closer to Brisbane, where the couple work(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy. Justin and Annabelle must get busy to complete rooms in time for a visit from Justin's 95-year-old grandmother. Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The first in a week of parties in Newcastle is hosted by mother and workaholic Clare, who aims to impress her guests with an Indian-themed menu of spicy pakoras and lamb curry in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Comedy promoter David hosts the second night in Newcastle, where he aims to impress his guests with a fine-dining menu featuring caviar, scallops, beef fillet and oysters, in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. However, some of the guests have reservations about the host, meaning he has his work cut out for him(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third night in Newcastle sees sophisticated marketing consultant Liz trying to impress her guests with a fancy French-themed menu. However, she spends most of her time trying to keep the peace as outspoken comedy promoter David annoys club singer John and rubs mum-of-three Clare up the wrong way with his snobbish views(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Club singer John hopes that a menu inspired by his travels around the world will help him win the £1,000 prize as he hosts the fourth night in Newcastle. He also has a musical surprise in store for his guests, but faces a challenge trying to impress David who refused to take part in the fun and games on Liz's evening. However, further rifts appear in the group when Vicky wants to discuss Geordie icons(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the night of the final dinner party in Newcastle and the turn of hotel administrator Vicky to entertain her guests. However, there is tension in the air and the spiritual host is challenged to keep the peace when John lets David know exactly what he thinks of him. The theme of negativity continues as mum-of-three Claire and Liz both take the comedy promoter to task over his questionable sense of humour, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is revealed(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. A young Asian elephant keeps the rest of the herd entertained as they adapt to the loss of two members to a virus. The keepers believe it needs a playmate its own age, but their hopes rest on the bull elephant mating with the females once more(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A champion running dog is admitted to see if Noel Fitzpatrick and the team can fix its abnormal elbow joint, and a couple have a nerve-racking wait while their Rhodesian ridgeback undergoes a high-risk operation to relieve the pressure on the nerves in its spine. Noel also treats an injured dog belonging to a friend from the gym(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to the Midlothian countryside to visit Pru and Richard Irvine, who planned to build a family home on an old industrial site. The biggest hurdles they faced were blending the property in with the landscape and working the long hours needed to manage the project. Kevin learns how this scheme fared and whether the locals' fears about an out-of-character building were proven valid(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The medics treat 60-year-old diabetic Tony, who has an infected leg wound and must be assessed to ensure it doesn't spread, and three-year-old Billy, who has swollen eyes after accidentally squirting detergent in them. Also treated is 80-year-old Ida, who has pain in her knee, and doctors are concerned when an x-ray reveals a shadow on her bone, which requires further tests to rule out cancer(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: A visiting journalist picks at the hospital's flaws while shadowing Max. Bloom and Reynolds have a major disagreement and Kapoor confronts issues from his past(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the famous quiz, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Jonathan Ross and Roisin Conaty. Countdown regular Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner with David O'Doherty, while maths whizz Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little 'help' from Joe Wilkinson(n)

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: A visiting journalist picks at the hospital's flaws while shadowing Max. Bloom and Reynolds have a major disagreement and Kapoor confronts issues from his past(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. A young Asian elephant keeps the rest of the herd entertained as they adapt to the loss of two members to a virus. The keepers believe it needs a playmate its own age, but their hopes rest on the bull elephant mating with the females once more(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of investigations from the Food Unwrapped casebook. Kate Quilton visits a cattle farm in America with supersized cows to feed the huge demand for beef, Jimmy Doherty discovers why pine nuts are so expensive, and Matt Tebbutt learns why shop-bought pies tend not to spill out their gravy - revealing that the answer grows on trees(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps George and Gillian Pook search for a holiday home and a property they can eventually retire to on Costa Rica's Caribbean coast, and shows them places she hopes will be suitable within their £100,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Having both retired from the Army, Rachel and Steven Grey are hoping to find the perfect property on Costa Rica's Pacific Costa Ballena where they can run a small B&B. Sara Damergi shows them a variety of possible options for their £250,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Anita and Cameron have a five-bedroom home in Donvale, Victoria, but feel the maze-like layout of the property stops them enjoying family time with their three children. Neale Whitaker must reunite the floor plan to persuade Anita to stay. Andrew Winter has his work cut out finding a house in the same area that Cameron thinks is better(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie finishes her mother Isobel's apartment. Painted and decorated, it now has her fathers framed pictures on the walls, a stunning 200-year-old mahogany canopy, plus furniture. Elsewhere, Jayne and Steve finish the Oriental Room and are ready to take in guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition visits the Nottingham and Derby area. American Hali kicks proceeding off with a travel-inspired menu, including Moroccan chicken cigars and an unusual dessert combination. With a very gregarious gathering of guests, the food plays second fiddle to a fun night of impressions, dance moves and kitchen chaos(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition continues in the Nottingham and Derby area. Car salesman Alistair hopes to step the competition up a gear. However, after narrowly avoiding an argument last night with Lesley, the veteran of the group, he's treading on thin ice. And he's serving up a bizarre menu, including chilli soup - for dessert - and prunes(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottingham, fun-loving funeral arranger Kim hosts an evening packed with games galore and a mixed bag menu. Fifty-seven-year-old Lesley is again the butt of car salesman Alistair's geriatric jibes and takes great delight in watching him put on the spot during a dance-off(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer relations associate and part-time drag queen Adam plans a theatrical night of flamboyance as the culinary competition continues in the Derby and Nottingham area. Can he get his guests begging for an encore, will Alastair and Lesley ever kiss and make up and what will Adam's guests think of his secret hobby as a ufologist?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final soiree in the Nottingham and Derby area sees 57-year-old Lesley counting on a night of glamour and glitz - plus some good home-cooked food - to secure the £1,000 prize. However, before any food is even dished up, the guests make a surprising discovery about the host(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry Cole makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill and Simon O'Brien creates a garden seat from an old mangle(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pedro the giant anteater is about to be reunited with long-term mate Bliss, but falls ill and faces a major operation, with an uncertain outcome. Chester Zoo's extremely rare bald ibises produce a batch of new chicks, which are destined to be released back into the wild in southern Spain. There is also a chance to witness the reunion of rhino couple Beni and Asha, who have been kept separate for four years(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A boxer dog is brought in with extensive injuries to its pelvis and hind legs from a road accident. Pioneering surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick considers a stem-cell scaffolding procedure - but there are significant risks attached. He and his team also help different animals with broken legs, including hedgehogs, and a kitten that has had an accident at home(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Andrew Tate and Deborah Mills, who participated in one of the programme's first projects. The pair tried to build a contemporary family home in Amersham, Buckinghamshire, and transform an adjacent water tower into a seven-storey bedroom wing. But while the modern building only took four months to complete, the tower conversion proved more difficult(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: Members of the Rocchi family are found dead in a hillside villa and all evidence points to a terrible case of homicide-suicide engineered by Sandro, the head of the clan. However the youngest, six-year-old Samuele, is found alive and unharmed in the boot of a car. In the light of the evidence, Valeria Ferro and the team consider whether a crime has been staged to mislead everyone. Miriam Leone stars. In Italian(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The 100th episode of the series focuses on the love between patients and their nearest and dearest. Sir John Cockburn, who's 89, arrives at St George's with acute abdominal pain and medics think it could be an abdominal aortic aneurysm and he faces emergency surgery. Meanwhile, 68-year-old Mick and his wife Linda have been involved in a head-on car crash. Mick took the brunt of the impact and has multiple injuries from top to toe that could be life-changing. The couple fell in love after leaving previous relationships, making a happy life together in their mobile home. And 10-year-old Niall is brought to A&E after falling 8ft from a tree on a family picnic. He's broken his arm so badly it looks like a banana, and orthopaedic doctors need to work out how to get it back in place(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old motorcyclist Sam is airlifted to St George's after crashing into a bus and being thrown under a car. He is placed in an induced coma and CT scans reveal Sam has bruising to his lungs, a ruptured kidney, a broken thigh bone and a fractured spine. The priority of the medics is is to stop his internal bleeding, while Sam's parents are rushed to the hospital by police. Meanwhile, seven-year-old Raheem arrives in A&E with his dad Dennis after cutting his wrist while playing at home, and 59-year-old Therese turns up with a painful lump on her finger(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pedro the giant anteater is about to be reunited with long-term mate Bliss, but falls ill and faces a major operation, with an uncertain outcome. Chester Zoo's extremely rare bald ibises produce a batch of new chicks, which are destined to be released back into the wild in southern Spain. There is also a chance to witness the reunion of rhino couple Beni and Asha, who have been kept separate for four years(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A boxer dog is brought in with extensive injuries to its pelvis and hind legs from a road accident. Pioneering surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick considers a stem-cell scaffolding procedure - but there are significant risks attached. He and his team also help different animals with broken legs, including hedgehogs, and a kitten that has had an accident at home(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton and Matt Tebbutt present more of their favourite investigations. Jimmy learns that Greek yoghurt production creates a potentially devastating by-product, Kate heads deep into the Amazon rainforest to find out what gives red Leicester cheese its colour and Matt asks why ostrich meat is red when other poultry is white(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps to bring a touch of romance and sophistication to Jannine and Shaun Cartwright's neglected bedroom(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Mike Bodnar and Liz Prendergast live on a houseboat in France for most of the year, but would like somewhere warmer for the winter months. They know the sun always shines in Tenerife, but are also drawn to Mike's native Liverpool as they have friends there and Liz could get work. Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi help them make up their minds(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Property experts Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi help Nathan and Georgina Spender, who are hoping to invest some money they inherited from their parents by downsizing in Leicestershire and buying a property in Lanzarote that they can initially use for holidays, with a view to eventually making it their permanent home(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Neil Smith and his partner Steph Grzyb regularly take holidays in Fuerteventura in the Canary Islands, where Neil can play golf in guaranteed sunshine. He would love to live there full-time, but Steph would rather settle on the Isle of Wight. Jasmine Harman and Sara Damergi are on hand to offer advice on the pros and cons of either move(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit two couples who were previously featured on the show. Sam and Krystle were keen to move from their rented flat in Deptford into their own place, while long-distance partners Jason and Jaye were looking for a one-bedroom pad with outside space in Clapham or Brixton to finally bring them together(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two couples to see if they managed to find their perfect property. First, Phil revisits civil partners Jo and Ally, who were house hunting in South London for a home big enough for three. Later, Phil also meets back up with Steve and Jo, who searched for a family home in Cardiff in 2015(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at David and Ewa Woodward's Keats Cottage in Shanklin on the Isle of Wight. In the rooms, Donna Taylor from Jasmine House in Leicestershire makes a hair-raising discovery, while mother-and-daughter team Lesley and Chloe Priestly take a dislike to one of their competitors at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Jasmine House in Lutterworth, Leicestershire managed by Donna Taylor who believes in a no frills approach. But it doesn't take long for Martin and Sara to realise all the added extras in the room don't add up. During the afternoon Leslie and Chloe show their fighting spirit during a kickboxing lesson and all the B&Bers square up to their host(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the luxury Halliwell House in Cheshire for the third day of the contest, where owners Martin Hulme and Sara Williams charge nearly £500 per night. Donna is astonished at the room rates, while David and Ewa are expecting nothing but perfection, but the self-service approach at breakfast confuses the guests, leaving Martin and Sara defiant in the face of their feedback(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at Soar Chapel Guest House in Ebbw Vale, the family home of mum and daughter Lesley and Chloe Priestly. Expectations are high for this duo and one guest in particular is not disappointed. Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog-lover Kerry Bolwell-Williams kicks off a week of dinner parties in the Welsh valleys, where she hopes that a full choir and a simple menu will impress her guests and make her an early contender for the £1,000 prize. However, she does not react well when it is suggested that her home has a rather distinctive smell(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Food-lover and theme park fan Dave Morris hosts the second dinner party in the Welsh valleys, hoping his home-grown herbs, hand-picked fruit and complicated menu will win him the £1,000 cash prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop owner Tasnim Sardar hosts the third night of the competition in the Welsh valleys, and hopes her Pakistani-inspired party will secure her the £1,000 prize. Financial advisor Gill Leonard likes her curries to be hot and spicy, and is particularly pleased by Tasnim's curried cauliflower(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ryan Woodward hosts on the fourth night in the Welsh valleys and has put a lot of thought into his evening, but each of his courses goes wrong - from a bread blunder to a clam crisis. He hopes an Ibizan dance lesson and drinks served in his hot tub will help to turn the party around(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in the Welsh valleys, financial advisor Gill Leonard dons the oven gloves to entertain the other contestants, and there is a dramatic start to the evening thanks to a mock fire-breathing dragon. Later, the host makes a remarkable revelation to her shocked guests(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a man who has had a seizure, but they are warned by the control room that the patient has a history of being abusive toward ambulance crews(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St George's Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her family's stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a project to build a car that can turn into a hovercraft and bring relief to flooded areas. programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme River Challenge
programme.desc: The naturalist travels down the Baliem River in New Guinea from its source to the sea, hoping to encounter the ancient tribes and dangerous wildlife that live long its banks and in the surrounding jungle. He begins his journey inland at Lake Habbema, and braves some of the world's fiercest white water rapids. Along the way, he stays in a village belonging to the Dani tribe and ventures into some previously unexplored caves(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, Lee and Lucy - who is three days overdue with their first baby - find themselves mistakenly caught up in a robbery at a local department store. Alongside them are Lee's dad, Frank, dressed as Santa's elf, Daisy and neighbours Toby and Anna. Locked in and faced with a dangerous armed robber wearing a Father Christmas mask, they have to come up with an escape plan. But will Lee's poorly conceived ideas get them into even more trouble? One-off Christmas special of the comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton(n)

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Terms and Conditions Apply
programme.desc: Using his trademark, powerpoint-comedy and a series of mischievous games, Dave invites three of the country's finest comic minds to help him make sense of our insensible world. In the first episode he is joined by Richard Osman, Phil Wang and Sara Barron, as they take a deep dive into the details we usually miss(n)

programme.title: Scream (1996)
programme.desc: The students of a high school are stalked by a masked knife-wielding maniac with a love of scary movies and a penchant for playing mind games with his prey. One of the girls on his hit list begins to suspect there is a connection between the killer and her mother's murder a year previously. Wes Craven's horror, which satirises the genre's cliches, starring Neve Campbell, David Arquette and Courteney Cox(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston journeys to Greece and Italy to uncover the truth about oracles - mythical figures who could reputedly hear and speak to gods(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the theory that Nazis were engaged in building spacecraft before the outbreak of the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson gets to choose a site for investigation and decides to take the team to Jersey, where a German anti-aircraft battery was built during the Second World War. As the experts explore the area, they realise it contained a large and complex settlement, and discover shocking facts about the islanders' suffering during the war(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Derwentcote in Co Durham, where they explore the Industrial Revolution's impact on the region by digging through the undergrowth to search for old furnaces and forges. Their investigation is also helped by the diary of an 18th-century industrial spy, and records from the time help shed light on how the area's cottage industries gave way to the power of expanding cities such as Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How the SAS War Crimes Investigation Team tried to pinpoint the location of Gestapo officers who were wanted for a host of atrocities(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Rion-Antirion Bridge, which runs for 3kms and links the town of Rio on the Peloponnese peninsula to Antirrio on mainland Greece. Located in one of Europe's most active seismic zones, its engineers had to conquer the problems of deep water, high winds and constant land mass movement to build the colossal structure(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Kevin Norris and Kieran Chisnall move three state-of-the-art trains, fresh in from Japan. They need to transport them from the North East port of Redcar to a factory in Doncaster(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Derwentcote in Co Durham, where they explore the Industrial Revolution's impact on the region by digging through the undergrowth to search for old furnaces and forges. Their investigation is also helped by the diary of an 18th-century industrial spy, and records from the time help shed light on how the area's cottage industries gave way to the power of expanding cities such as Sheffield(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Documentary examining the plants and animals that thrive in monsoon areas, narrated by Colin Salmon. Crested black macaques take advantage of an abundance of fruit, and maleo birds use the sun to incubate their eggs on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. On Java, locals climb volcanoes to offer live sacrifices in thanks for the peaks' help in precipitating rain and fertilising plants with ash. The frequent and heavy rains in Borneo have destroyed the soils, created fierce competition for scarce nutrients, and driven evolution to produce some curious creatures. The Rajah Brooke's pitcher encourages tree shrews to relieve themselves to obtain the substances it needs, while the Bornean red leech is capable of swallowing the giant blue earthworm in one gulp(n)

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: Prince John: The Windsors' Tragic Secret
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of King George V's youngest child, unravelling some of the mysteries and misconceptions surrounding him. Born in 1904, Prince John was diagnosed as epileptic and died at an early age - had he lived, he would have become the present Queen's uncle. Here, new research and photographic evidence are combined to build up a picture of his life(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team search for Roman artefacts beneath the graveyard of St Kyneburgha's Church in Castor, Cambridgeshire. With several reports of similar finds in and around the village, speculation mounts that they could uncover the remnants of a praetorium - a huge building that would have dominated the region's skyline during the period. However, as the experts start digging in other nearby locations, they discover that not all the evidence points to the same conclusion(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Groby Old Hall in Leicestershire, formerly the home of Edward IV's wife Elizabeth Woodville, and discover a surprising array of archaeological remains beneath its gardens, including a medieval wall and possible evidence of a Norman castle. They are joined by author Philippa Gregory, who delves into the complex history of the Greys, the house's former owners and one of the most powerful dynasties in medieval Britain(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: To survive the bone-chilling cold, deadly blizzards and darkness of the eight months of an Alaskan winter takes courage, cunning and remarkable endurance. This episode tells the stories of how the tough and resourceful animal and human inhabitants of the windswept mountain peaks, icy tundra and snowbound forests face up to the challenges of the season(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship that has developed over thousands of years between the people who live in monsoon regions and the wildlife they share them with. In the rice paddies of Assam, India, farmers battle a herd of hungry elephants over their crops, while on the Philippine island of Palawan, near Borneo, people still hunt in the forest and live in caves for part of the year. The programme also looks at the impact of organised religion, meeting members of the Bishnoi sect who believe that all life is sacred and have even been known to lay down their lives to protect trees. Colin Salmon narrates(n)

A German journalist persuaded his editor to finance a quest to reveal what was in boxes dumped by a group of SS officers toward the end of the Second World War. programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the effects of the eruption of volcanos in the ocean. From these violent beginnings emerge islands and coral reefs that are home to a vast array of wildlife, including giant manta rays and vampire bugs that thrive in the tropical snow, after a process that takes millions of years. programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: The struggle for survival on the region's isolated islands, home to flightless parrots, burrowing bats, giant skinks and tree-dwelling kangaroos. programme.title: South Pacific

programme.title: The British Tribe Next Door
programme.desc: Scarlett Moffatt and her family are plucked from the UK and transplanted into a replica of their terraced family home - complete with running water, electricity and their possessions around them - slap bang in the middle of a Namibian tribal village. They are to stay in Otjeme village, home to 100 semi-nomadic Himba cattle herders, who choose to live very traditional lives, but are overwhelmed by what their first impressions. programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Dispatches investigates the extraordinary friendship between Prince Andrew and multimillionaire paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. As the full extent of Epstein's crimes emerge, the Prince stands accused of sleeping with a 17-year-old girl supplied by the investor. As the quest to locate the missing artwork gets under way, the agents realise they need the help of an expert. programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Matt Allwright present the third of four occasional documentaries profiling Britain's everyday heroes as they battle the challenges arising from the different times of year. programme.title: Doctors In The War Zone
programme.desc: The United Nations estimates almost 200,000 people have been killed since Syria's civil war began, with many hundreds of thousands more injured. Millions have been forced from their homes, but the full human cost of the conflict is little known to the outside world because of the danger Western journalists face in trying to enter areas not controlled by the government. Most doctors have fled from those locations and many hospitals have been destroyed, but this documentary follows British trauma surgeon David Nott as he makes the perilous journey across the border into Syria to work alongside medics who have stayed behind. He carries out life-saving surgery in a facility forced underground by daily bombardment(n)

programme.title: What's Cooking? (2000)
programme.desc: The members of four diverse families meet up in their respective homes to celebrate Thanksgiving, where the growing Americanisation of the younger generation becomes ever more apparent to their parents, and tangled emotional battles are played out. Drama exploring multiculturalism in Los Angeles, starring Dennis Haysbert, Joan Chen, Julianna Margulies and Kyra Sedgewick(n)

programme.title: The Harder They Come (1973)
programme.desc: A naive young man from rural Jamaica heads for Kingston in search of reggae stardom, only to get involved in the shady world of drug dealing as his musical career takes off. With no other option but to go on the run, he fights back against the system which forced him into a life of crime and becomes a political hero in the process. Crime drama, starring Jimmy Cliff, Janet Barkley, Carl Bradshaw and Bob Charlton(n)

programme.title: Queensbury Boxing League
programme.desc: Boxers battle it out over three, four and five two minute rounds against opponents - the most successful competitors will earn the right to challenge for the coveted Queensbury British title.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Joey Bones selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene(n)

programme.title: Keeping Britain Safe 24/7
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury and Matt Allwright profile the people battling the unexpected challenges arising from the summer months. The film goes behind the scenes of the safety teams at airports as they are overrun with holiday-makers, follows the police who ensure music festivals go off without any trouble, and introduces the army of workers left to clean up the mess when the party is over in Notting Hill(n)

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days.(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: Interviews on news and events from across London.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls investigate a plan to rig a male beauty contest by transforming the finalists(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: John Peter invents a cure for tiredness, but it ends up turning people into zombies(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvbar goes in search of a shooting star, but finds it is nothing but a worthless rock. However, Vic works out that it may have very useful magnetic properties(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar's ship runs aground in the fog and a rival crew steals his loot. Vic tries to get the treasure back by convincing the thieves it has been cursed by the god of thunder(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra writes a play about a brave animal and a princess(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: McMeer causes trouble after getting caught in Zigby's giant kite(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Vic tries to scare off the tax collector by tricking him into thinking there are monsters lurking near the village(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is kidnapped by his arch rival Sven, and Vic has to find a way to make the ship move without a sail(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The zebra builds a factory when McMeer's runs out of coconut cake(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The lively zebra looks after Bertie, who is having a clumsy day(n)

programme.title: King Alfred and the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Michael Wood explores the role of Alfred the Great's grandson Aethelstan in creating a kingdom of all England. The presenter travels to Devon, Cumbria, Scotland and Rome to reveal why he believes the English monarch was one of the greatest, and tells the tale of the ruler's learning and law-making, including his development of a national coinage and his defeat of the Vikings and Scots in the Anglo-Saxon Great War(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok(n)

programme.title: Doctors In The War Zone
programme.desc: The United Nations estimates almost 200,000 people have been killed since Syria's civil war began, with many hundreds of thousands more injured. Millions have been forced from their homes, but the full human cost of the conflict is little known to the outside world because of the danger Western journalists face in trying to enter areas not controlled by the government. Most doctors have fled from those locations and many hospitals have been destroyed, but this documentary follows British trauma surgeon David Nott as he makes the perilous journey across the border into Syria to work alongside medics who have stayed behind. He carries out life-saving surgery in a facility forced underground by daily bombardment(n)

programme.title: A Kind of Loving (1962)
programme.desc: A factory worker gets his girlfriend pregnant, forcing them to endure years of misery and dashed hopes as they marry and move in with her disapproving, nagging mother. John Schlesinger's drama, starring Alan Bates, June Ritchie and Thora Hird(n)

programme.title: The Boy Who Stole a Million (1960)
programme.desc: A boy robs a bank to pay for repairs on his father's car, and is pursued by the police and criminals after the money. Comedy adventure, starring Virgilio Teixeira and Maurice Reyna(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow embarks on a grime-filled journey through the histories of three of the world's leading modern cities. He begins with London, using CGI footage and re-enactments to experience life in the capital in the 14th century. He spends a night as a medieval muck-raker, and tries on a pair of wooden platform shoes designed to elevate the wearer above the filth that covered the streets(n)

programme.title: Royally Mad
programme.desc: The most ardent American fans of the royal family take a trip round London in the run-up to Prince William's wedding(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: Investigating one of the worst air disasters for two decades, Malaysia Airlines flight MH17 from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur, which crashed, killing 298 passengers on July 17, 2014. It occurred four months after the disappearance of Malaysian Airlines flight MH370, and has led to speculation that only a powerful ground-to-air missile could be responsible, but there are eyewitness accounts of other aircrafts seen flying next to MH17 close to impact. This documentary tracks down eye witnesses, and speaks with secret intelligence sources in an attempt to sort fact from fiction(n)

programme.title: The Flowers of War (2011)
programme.desc: An American drifter arrives in Nanjing in 1937, just as Japanese forces invade. Hiding out in a Western-run church, he poses as a priest, but ends up adopting his role and providing shelter for orphans and courtesans as carnage unfolds in the city. Drama, starring Christian Bale and Ni Ni. In Nanjing dialect, English, Japanese, Mandarin and Shanghainese(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
